title: M3 Design Document - Technical Architecture and Implementation Plan
description: Comprehensive design document for M3 milestone including: Dashboard architecture (React + Express + WebSocket), Worker/Shadow agent role-based design, DaemonAgent command registry pattern, component interfaces, data models, error handling, and testing strategy.
keyPoints: 4 major components with detailed interfaces and architecture
Code reuse analysis for existing components
Mermaid architecture diagram showing component relationships
Data models for RoleConfig, ShadowRoleConfig, CommandDefinition
reviewNotes:
Please review the design document and verify that:
1. Architecture aligns with tech.md and structure.md
2. Component interfaces are clear and well-defined
3. Code reuse analysis identifies existing components correctly
4. Data models are appropriate for requirements
5. Error handling covers all critical scenarios
6. Testing strategy is comprehensive and achievable
7. Implementation plan is realistic for 14 days
8. Migration path is safe and incremental
revisionNotes:
Revisions made based on feedback:

1. Fixed architecture diagram: Removed incorrect ArcadeDB connection from mcp-server-quest. Clarified that agent-producer and agent-worker are responsible for persisting data to ArcadeDB, not mcp-server-quest.

2. Added deployment flexibility section: Clarified that mcp-client-quest can be deployed on remote DigitalOcean droplet. Explained that the architecture supports flexible deployment (local, remote, or hybrid) because all components communicate via KĀDI broker (location-independent).

Key clarifications:
- mcp-server-quest is a stateless MCP server that provides tools (no direct ArcadeDB access)
- agent-producer and agent-worker persist quest/task data to ArcadeDB after invoking mcp-server-quest tools
- Dashboard can be deployed anywhere as long as it can reach KĀDI broker
- WebSocket works over HTTP/HTTPS (supports remote access)
